fixes.code:
import sys
from sortedcontainers import SortedList

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    idx = 0
    t = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
    
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        a = [int(input_data[idx+i]) for i in range(n)]
        idx += n
        
        sl = SortedList()
        total = 0
        
        for x in a:
            if not sl:
                sl.add(x)
                continue
            
            # cost to add to front: number of elements < x (they'll be to the right, x > them means inversions... wait)
            # front: x goes to leftmost position, elements to its right are all current elements
            # inversion if x > element_to_right, so cost = count of elements < x
            front_cost = sl.bisect_left(x)  # count of elements strictly less than x
            
            # back: x goes to rightmost position, elements to its left are all current elements  
            # inversion if element_to_left > x, so cost = count of elements > x
            back_cost = len(sl) - sl.bisect_right(x)  # count of elements strictly greater than x
            
            total += min(front_cost, back_cost)
            sl.add(x)
        
        print(total)

solve()

import sys
from bisect import insort, bisect_left, bisect_right

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    idx = 0
    t = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
    
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        a = [int(input_data[idx+i]) for i in range(n)]
        idx += n
        
        sl = []
        total = 0
        
        for x in a:
            if not sl:
                insort(sl, x)
                continue
            
            # cost to add to front: number of elements < x (they'll be to the right, x > them means inversions)
            front_cost = bisect_left(sl, x)  # count of elements strictly less than x
            
            # back: x goes to rightmost position, elements to its left are all current elements  
            # inversion if element_to_left > x, so cost = count of elements > x
            back_cost = len(sl) - bisect_right(sl, x)  # count of elements strictly greater than x
            
            total += min(front_cost, back_cost)
            insort(sl, x)
        
        print(total)

solve()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys
from bisect import insort, bisect_left, bisect_right

def solve():
    input_data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    idx = 0
    t = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
    
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(input_data[idx]); idx += 1
        a = [int(input_data[idx+i]) for i in range(n)]
        idx += n
        
        sl = []
        total = 0
        
        for x in a:
            if not sl:
                insort(sl, x)
                continue
            
            # cost to add to front: number of elements < x (they'll be to the right, x > them means inversions)
            front_cost = bisect_left(sl, x)  # count of elements strictly less than x
            
            # back: x goes to rightmost position, elements to its left are all current elements  
            # inversion if element_to_left > x, so cost = count of elements > x
            back_cost = len(sl) - bisect_right(sl, x)  # count of elements strictly greater than x
            
            total += min(front_cost, back_cost)
            insort(sl, x)
        
        print(total)

solve()
